DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-31-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Nicole Jones (415-362-2580) from Lewis, Brisbois, Bisgaard, & Smith appearing for defendant and opposing party./ No other apperances.

Defendant did not properly notice the moving party of their apperance today. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF CARMEN VALDEZ-MADRANOS' DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with 10 Days LEAVE TO AMEND to allege ultimate facts in support of affirmative defenses. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Bautista, Not reported (D501)

Amended Re-Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Benjamin Martin, Esq. (415-981-9290) of Law Offices of Benjamin Martin, appearing through court call for Carmen Valdez-Madranos, Plaintiff and moving party / Nicole L. Meredith, Esq. (415-398-0200) for Michael S. Burke, Defendant and opposing party

ARGUED by Mr. Martin on behalf of the Plaintiff and moving party. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF CARMEN VALDEZ-MADRANOS' Amended Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED. Motion is untimely per CCP 2031.310, also see Sperber v. Robinson (1994) 26 Cal.App.4th 736. No sanctions.. Defendant's counsel to submit proposed order conforming to the Tentative Ruling. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

Renotice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:Benjamin Martin, Esq. (415-981-9290) for Carmen Valdez-Madranos, plaintiff and moving party / Michael Burke, Esq. (415-398-0200) for Meridian Management Group, defendant and opposing party

At 9:35am, in open court, plaintiff's counsel reports to the Court that defendant's counsel refused to meet and confer re the motion. Defendant's counsel responds as to the untimely filed plaintiff's motion. Defendant's counsel is admonished by Court for not following the order to "meet and confer". Plaintiff's counsel argues on the untimely filing of its motion. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: PLAINTIFF CARMEN VALDEZ-MADRANOS' Renotice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions is taken off calendar without prejudice. Court denies plaintiff's counsel's request to retrieve the courtesy copies given to the Court for this motion. Judge Peter J. Busch, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
